offBrand: brand/email/SYSTEM.md + all 5 emails: the dye palette is INVENTED and contradicts the live tokens the brand book claims to mirror. Live globals.css: --dye-madder #d94f38, --dye-cochineal #8a5578 (purple-toned), --dye-madder-text(dark) #d94f38. Emails ship madder thread #b5443a, cochineal #9a2f4f (wine-red), madder-text #e07a6a. None match. Two deliverables both claim 'verbatim from live globals.css' yet ship different hex — the family identity colours (the brand's #1 distinctive asset) are not the same colour across email vs website vs product.

brand/email/*.html (welcome, kin-joined, entry-unlocked, death-verification): dye name colour fails AA in light mode. A single static {{dye_text}} placeholder is injected (dark-tuned pastel, e.g. #e07a6a / #ecc06a). Under @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) the surface flips to #efe2cc/#f6ecda but the dye name colour does NOT swap, so the pastel name text fails 4.5:1 on the light ground. The brand's own dual --dye/--dye-text (light scope) promise is unmet in email — a real a11y regression for the multigenerational reader.

brand/email/entry-unlocked.html: the after-passing release — the brand's MOST protected object — branches only via an HTML comment. Static HTML cannot branch on {{release_kind}}; as shipped it renders the EMBER CTA (#e0a062) and the marketing-chrome treatment for a passing release. That is the single worst doNot violation in the system (ember + marketing on the after-passing email) sitting one un-actioned comment away from going live.

brand/identity/favicon.svg + icon.svg: the master mark's interlace is single-direction. Outer warps are always drawn first (pass under) and the centre warp always last (passes over); a real weave alternates over/under per cell. The favicon has no centre warp at all, so its 'over-under' reads as one weft simply laid across two warps — closer to a plus/hash silhouette at 16px than a true woven knot. Matches the brief's literal coordinates but the brief itself encodes a non-alternating weave.

brand/book/CONCEPT.md: ember appears on the cover as the knot foil AND the wordmark is specified as 'copper foil' (§cover, lines 57/72). Copper-foil wordmark = ember used as a large identity surface, exceeding the 'ember under 5%, two surfaces only (knot foil + one colophon rule)' rule the same document states on line 100. Internal contradiction; the wordmark should be blind deboss or bone/ink foil, not copper.
gaps: brand/BRAND.md DOES NOT EXIST on disk. The make:brandbook deliverable claims a 20876-byte authoritative constitution plus four SVGs at brand/mark/*.svg — none are present. brand/ contains only book/ email/ identity/ social/ website/. The central reference every other deliverable cites ('Read the Brand System first', 'per the brand voiceRules') is missing, so there is no single source of truth on disk. This is the largest gap.

No design tokens / no machine-readable artifact. There is no tokens.json, CSS, or Figma-equivalent export — the dye-hex drift (email vs live vs website) exists precisely because nothing forces one canonical colour table. A shared dye table (10 dyes × {thread, text-dark, text-light} × verified ratios) should be the one file all six deliverables import.

No verified light-mode dye-text table anywhere in the email or social deliverables. SYSTEM.md §4 ships only the on-dark dye-text column; the parchment/light --dye-*-text values already exist in globals.css but were never carried into email or the social Dye Card spec.

Social and Book reference 'the Family Spectrum' and 'choosing your thread' but no deliverable specifies the actual generation rule (order, max bands, what a 30-member family's spectrum collapses to). The coat-of-arms asset is asserted across 4 deliverables with no single spec.

No wordmark outline/production note resolved: wordmark.svg and all email lockups depend on live Fraunces with a Georgia/Hoefler fallback. There is no shipped outlined-glyph wordmark, so the lockup silently renders in a serif fallback wherever Fraunces is absent (most email clients, the Book proof) — acknowledged in the index note but not delivered.

No plain-text email parts and no real subject-line set for the five emails (SYSTEM.md defers plain-text to 'the sending system'); the death-verification and check-in especially need crafted, non-alarming subject lines specified, not left to integration.
fixes: Create one canonical dye table file (e.g. brand/tokens/dyes.json) generated from cloudflare/frontend/src/styles/globals.css with all 30 values: dark --dye-* (thread) and --dye-*-text, plus parchment --dye-* and --dye-*-text, each with its measured contrast ratio. Then rewrite the email SYSTEM.md §4 table and every {{dye_*}} placeholder to use THOSE exact hex. Verify: madder thread must be #d94f38, cochineal #8a5578, not the invented #b5443a/#9a2f4f.

Email: make dye name colour theme-aware. Inject two placeholders ({{dye_text_dark}} and {{dye_text_light}}) and add a per-email .dye-name class whose color is set on dark and overridden in the @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) block to the parchment --dye-*-text value — exactly as the brand's dual-token rule requires. Re-run a 4.5:1 check against #efe2cc for all 10.

Split entry-unlocked.html into two real files: entry-unlocked-date.html (ember CTA kept) and release-passing.html (no ember, no wordmark lockup, plain bone link, 'Someone left this for you. It opens now.'). Do not rely on an HTML comment to remove ember — static email cannot branch. Document that the worker selects the file by release_kind.

Author brand/BRAND.md for real (the missing constitution) and move the four mark SVGs to brand/mark/ as claimed — or correct the deliverable index to point at brand/identity/. Right now the index lies about both the file and its byte count. Make BRAND.md import the canonical dye table so it can never drift again.

Book: change the cover wordmark from 'copper foil' to blind deboss (or bone/ink foil), leaving the ember knot pip as the only copper-foil hit, so the cover obeys the document's own 'ember on exactly two surfaces' rule.

Favicon: add the alternating-weave cue at 16px — either give it a centre warp drawn over (so one weft passes under, one over) or thicken the gap so the over/under read survives; otherwise it collapses toward a hash. Optionally apply true alternating over/under in the 192 master so the mark is a real weave, not three parallel warps with a constant centre-over.

Add crafted subject lines + plain-text parts to the email deliverable, especially check-in ('A quiet note — no action needed') and death-verification ('{{member_first}} asked us to reach you') so the non-alarming voice survives in the inbox preview, not just the body.
verdict: rework — The creative DNA is genuinely strong and on-brand: the voice across email, social, website, and book is disciplined and reverent (no urgency, arrival-of-love framing, kept-vocabulary held throughout), the warp-and-weft mark is ownable and not the retired cosmic-node cliché, and the website REDESIGN.md is implementation-grade and token-honest. But this cannot ship as one coherent brand yet for three hard reasons. (1) The authoritative brand book (brand/BRAND.md) and its four claimed mark SVGs are simply not on disk, so there is no single source of truth and the deliverable index misreports what exists. (2) The family-identity dyes — the brand's #1 distinctive asset — are three different colour sets across email vs live tokens vs the book/website claims, because nothing enforces one canonical table; the email palette is invented and contradicts the live globals.css it says it copied. (3) The most-protected object in the entire system, the after-passing release email, is one un-actioned HTML comment away from rendering with an ember CTA and marketing chrome, and the email dye names fail AA in light mode. Fix the canonical dye table, split the release email into real files, restore BRAND.md, and resolve the book cover ember — then it ships as a coherent, premium, contemporary system.
